California
Fair Political
Practices Commission
August 25,
1992
Eileen M.
Child
Treasurer,
Citizens Allied for a
MultiPurpose Unified School
999-C Edgewater Blvd.
#317
Foster City,
CA
94404
Re:
Your Request for Confirmation
of Telephone Advice
Our File No.
A-92-448
Dear Ms.
Child:
This is in response to your request for confirmation of
telephone advice concerning the campaign disclosure provisions of
the Political Reform Act
(the “Act”).
1
This will confirm my advice to you that the activities of
Citizens Allied for a MultiPurpose Unified School
(CAMPUS)
have
not resulted in the organization qualifying as a committee under
the Act.
I advised you to file a Statement of Termination
(Form
415)
with the Secretary of State.
If the unification measure is approved by the State Depart
ment of Education for placement on the ballot and
CAMPUS begins
raising funds to support the measure, it will incur filing obliga
tions as a committee if it receives or makes contributions total
ing $1,000 or more,
or makes independent expenditures totaling
$1,000 or more,
during a calendar year.
1
Government Code Sections 81000—91015.
All statutory references
are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated.
Commission
regulations appear at
2 California Code of Regulations Section
18000,
et
All references to regulations are to Title 2,
Division
6 of the California Code of Regulations.
428 J Street. Suite 800
•
P.O. Box 807
•
Sacramento, CA
95812
•
(916) 322-5660


Eileen
M.
Child

Page
2
If you have any further questions concerning this matter,
please contact me at
(916)
322—5662.
Sincerely,
Scott Hallabrin
Assistant Chief Counsel

sections.conclusion: This will confirm my advice to you that the activities of Citizens Allied for a MultiPurpose Unified School (CAMPUS) have not resulted in the organization qualifying as a committee under the Act. I advised you to file a Statement of Termination (Form 415) with the Secretary of State. If the unification measure is approved by the State Department of Education for placement on the ballot and CAMPUS begins raising funds to support the measure, it will incur filing obligations as a committee if it receives or makes contributions totaling $1,000 or more, or makes independent expenditures totaling $1,000 or more, during a calendar year.
sections.question_synthetic: Whether Citizens Allied for a MultiPurpose Unified School (CAMPUS) qualifies as a committee under the Political Reform Act's campaign disclosure provisions, and what filing obligations it may have.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: FPPC confirms that CAMPUS has not qualified as a committee under current activities and should file a Statement of Termination. However, if the organization begins raising funds to support a ballot measure, it will have filing obligations once contributions or independent expenditures reach $1,000 or more in a calendar year.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Confirmation of prior telephone advice letter with clear Q/C elements embedded in body text rather than in formal sections. Document reference number A-92-448 confirms this is advice letter 92-448.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Whether Citizens Allied for a MultiPurpose Unified School (CAMPUS) qualifies as a committee under the Political Reform Act's campaign disclosure provisions, and what filing obligations it may have.

CONCLUSION: This will confirm my advice to you that the activities of Citizens Allied for a MultiPurpose Unified School (CAMPUS) have not resulted in the organization qualifying as a committee under the Act. I advised you to file a Statement of Termination (Form 415) with the Secretary of State. If the unification measure is approved by the State Department of Education for placement on the ballot and CAMPUS begins raising funds to support the measure, it will incur filing obligations as a committee if it receives or makes contributions totaling $1,000 or more, or makes independent expenditures totaling $1,000 or more, during a calendar year.

I advised you to file a Statement of Termination (Form 415) with the Secretary of State. If the unification measure is approved by the State Depart ment of Education for placement on the ballot and CAMPUS begins raising funds to support the measure, it will incur filing obliga tions as a committee if it receives or makes contributions total ing $1,000 or more, or makes independent expenditures totaling $1,000 or more, during a calendar year. 1 Government Code Sections 81000—91015. All statutory references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated. embedding.summary: FPPC confirms telephone advice to CAMPUS treasurer that the organization has not qualified as a committee under the Political Reform Act and should file Form 415 for termination, while noting future filing obligations if the organization raises funds for a ballot measure.
